core_themes.theme: 人的存在本身就是一种审判和惩罚
excerpts: Kafka's novels portray in lucid, deceptively simple prose a man's desperate struggle with an overwhelming power, one that may persecute its victim (as in The Trial) or one that may be sought after and begged in vain for approval (as in The Castle).
K. never discovered why he was arrested or what he was charged with, and he was never able to understand the principles governing the system of justice in which he found himself ensnared.
core_themes.source: Britannica - Franz Kafka Biography / The Trial

core_themes.theme: 个人与压迫性权威之间不可调和的冲突
excerpts: The conflict with the father is reflected directly in Kafka's story Das Urteil (1913; The Judgment). It is projected on a grander scale in Kafka's novels.
Kafka's father had a profound impact on both Kafka's life and writing. He was a tyrant of sorts, with a wicked temper and little appreciation for his son's creative side.

core_themes.theme: 语言的局限性和沟通的根本失败
excerpts: The source of Kafka's despair lies in a sense of ultimate isolation from true communion with all human beings—the friends he cherished, the women he loved, the job he detested, the society he lived in—and with God.
He felt his will had been broken by his father.

core_themes.theme: 现代生活的荒谬性——理性与无意义并存
excerpts: This surreal story of a young man who finds himself caught up in the mindless bureaucracy of the law has become synonymous with the anxieties and alienation of the modern age.
So alive and vibrant were the tales that Kafka spun about man and faceless organizations that a new term was introduced into the English lexicon: 'Kafkaesque.'

core_themes.theme: 内疚感不依赖于实际的罪行——审判先于犯罪
excerpts: The priest then tells him a baffling parable. On the eve of K.'s 31st birthday, two men come to his home. He goes with them, and they hold his arms. Although it seems that they are going where K. leads them, they take him to an abandoned quarry and have him sit with his head on a stone.
In the end, he did not resist his inevitable execution.

core_themes.theme: 家庭作为压迫性权威的原型
excerpts: In Kafka's most important attempt at autobiography, Brief an den Vater (written 1919; Letter to Father), a letter that never reached the addressee, Kafka attributed his failure to live, to cut loose from parental ties and establish himself in marriage and fatherhood, as well as his escape into literature, to the prohibitive father figure.
He felt his will had been broken by his father. Writing to Brod, Franz Kafka mentioned that he debated suicide because of the pressures his family placed on him.

philosophical_annotations.existentialism: Simone de Beauvoir, who considered The Castle a pioneering work of existentialism, said of it, 'Kafka spoke to us about ourselves.'
philosophical_annotations.absurdism: One of the few stories authored by Kafka that was published during his lifetime, following The Judgment (1913) and preceding In the Penal Colony (1919).
philosophical_annotations.alienation: Isolation—from companionship, from intimacy, from fulfillment—is a theme in both Kafka's life and his works.

summary: 卡夫卡的思想内核围绕'人在压迫性权威面前的无力与异化'这一主题展开。他描绘了现代人在官僚体系、家庭和社会权威面前的困境：无论是否有罪，人总是被审判；无论多么努力，沟通总是失败；无论多么渴望归属，人总是孤立。这种存在性的焦虑通过精确、清醒的语言表达出来，成为20世纪存在主义和荒诞主义的文学先驱。
